ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Ngwadaa nemɔ lili apereapereɛ wɔ ye kunu, na ɔhane kyɛ, “Sɛ ɔte sɔ de a, nzaade nzikyɛ dwirɛ yeɛ wato me ye?” Yiti ɔhɔle AWURADE sona berɛ oobisale so.
translated: The woman had a small child who was crying, and she said, "If I go to the river and the crocodile seizes me, who will take care of my child?" She then prayed to the Lord to have mercy on her.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: Ɔtole ye dumaa Nowa, na ɔhane kyɛ, “Ye yeɛ okofi aseɛ bɔ AWURADE anoma ye ne anu ɔkɔkyekye yɛ wora wɔ yɛ adwuma, ɔne ɛfɛɛ wora bɔ yɛfɛ yɛ sa nu ɔ.”
translated: The eagle said to the hen, and she also said, "You are the one who is going to carry the Lord's message to his people to go and work, and he will also come and carry us to where we will go."

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: “Fi ɛlɛen alaka ne anu dwu ase, wɔ, ɛye, wɔ mma mɔ ne bɛ ye mɔ.
translated: When the palm nut falls, it doesn't fall alone, others go with it, and some will go and some will not go.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Ɔ́ma wɔ ne wɔ bosofoɛ bɛ sa ha Abraham saleɛ ne, amaa ɔnate so ɛmɔ sa aha aseɛ bɔ yeɛ Nyameɛ fa mane Abraham, bɔ yeɛ aayɛ so ɛwɔfoɛ ye.”
translated: He said to him, "If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, they will not be convinced even if someone rises from the dead to tell them to repent to God as Abraham did, to do what is good in the eyes of the Lord as Abraham did, and to do what is right in the sight of men."

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Na AWURADE sele Abram kyɛ, “Tu fi wɔ maen nu, w’abusuafoɛ nu, ɔne ɛse awuro, ná kɔ aseɛ bɔ mebaahyire wɔ ne aso.
translated: The Lord said to Abram, "Leave your country, your people and your father's household and go to the land I will show you."
